description_long: This is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain and some visible wear, suggesting frequent use. The handle is medium brown and tapers gently towards the end, providing a comfortable grip. The axe head is made of solid metal, likely steel, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a slightly rounded poll on the opposite side. The blade has a single sharp edge and a matte, weathered finish, showing signs of use but no significant rust or damage. The head is mounted securely onto the handle, with a visible wooden wedge at the top to keep it in place. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A traditional hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, single-edged steel blade, showing signs of use and a secure, wedged mounting.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the side, highlighting the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped steel blade.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the top, showing the rectangular cross-section of the handle and the thickness of the blade, with the wooden wedge visible at the head.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the other face of the blade and handle, with similar wear and curvature as the first side.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the front, displaying the flat face of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle behind it.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, medium brown color, oval cross-section, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a broad cutting edge and a slightly rounded poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a visible wooden wedge securing the blade to the handle.
